title: Example
text:
Kaplan-Meier: 90-Day Survival by HRS Reversal


Code
fit <- survfit(
  Surv(time_to_death_90days, time_to_death_status_90days) ~ HRS_reversal_new,
  data = final_master
)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = final_master,
  surv.scale = "percent",
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(75, 1),
  pval.size = 4,
  conf.int = FALSE,
  xlim = c(0, 90),
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  break.time.by = 30,
  tables.theme = theme_cleantable(),
  risk.table = TRUE,
  risk.table.y.text.col = TRUE,
  risk.table.y.text = TRUE,
  risk.table.fontsize = 4
)











Logistic Regression: 90-Day Mortality
Multivariate logistic regression with HRS reversal as primary exposure, adjusted for age, race, ethnicity, MELD 3.0, and transplant listing status.


Code
model_logistic <- glm(
  time_to_death_status_90days ~
    HRS_reversal_new + age + race_2 + hispanic + admit_meld_3 + listed_transplant,
  family = binomial,
  data = final_master
)

tbl_regression(model_logistic, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  add_n() %>%
  bold_p(0.05) %>%
  bold_labels()

Fine-Gray Competing Risk: 90-Day Mortality (Death vs. Transplant)
Multivariate Fine-Gray competing risk model. Death (failcode = 1) is the event of interest; transplant (failcode = 2) is the competing event; cencode = 0 is censored.


Code
model_fg <- tidycmprsk::crr(
  Surv(time_to_death_90days_cmp, time_to_death_status_90days_cmp) ~
    HRS_reversal_new + age + race_2 + hispanic + admit_meld_3,
  failcode = 1,
  cencode = 0,
  data = final_master
)

tbl_regression(model_fg, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  add_n() %>%
  bold_p(0.05) %>%

title: Project Summary
text: The HARMONY Terlipressin Registry is a multi-center retrospective study of terlipressin use for hepatorenal syndrome-acute kidney injury (HRS-AKI). Data were collected from 15 centers via REDCap and analyzed in R.

title: Project Summary
text:
Data Pipeline





flowchart TD
    A[15 REDCap CSVs<br/>Partners HealthCare Dropbox] --> B[Read & assign center IDs]
    B --> C[Alphabetize columns<br/>Drop inconsistent vars]
    C --> D[reorder_and_bind<br/>Merge all centers]
    D --> E[qc_check<br/>Find Discont_Day & missing data]
    E --> F[Remove excluded subjects<br/>34 removed, 8 re-treatment]
    F --> G[Derive clinical scores<br/>Child-Pugh, CLIF-C, MELD]
    G --> H[Compute outcomes<br/>HRS reversal, survival, transplant]
    H --> I[final_master dataset]
    I --> J[Table 1 / Stratified tables]
    I --> K[Survival & competing risk models]
    I --> L[Multiple imputation<br/>revision 0311.Rmd]

text:

Cleaned final dataset


Terlipressin Database _ REDCap Code Book.pdf
REDCap data dictionary (132 pages, 1500+ fields)

title: Project Summary
text:
Exclusion Criteria

Removed subjects (n=34): 7 BID-PTD patients, 24 MAYO patients (IDs 5–28), 3 MMC patients (IDs 005–007)
Re-treatment subjects (n=8): Separated into subgroup_master – BID-005R1/R2, BID-013R1, MGH-001R1, MGH-014R1/R2, MGH-026R1, NWM-014R1
Patients without a determined Discont_Day are also excluded (filter(!is.na(Discont_Day)))

title: Data Summary
text:
get_common_and_missing_variables
Identifies shared and center-specific columns across all dataframes.


Code
get_common_and_missing_variables <- function(...) {
  dfs <- list(...)
  args <- match.call(expand.dots = FALSE)$`...`
  df_names <- sapply(args, deparse)
  df_cols <- lapply(dfs, names)
  common_cols <- Reduce(intersect, df_cols)
  all_cols <- unique(unlist(df_cols))
  missing_cols <- lapply(df_cols, function(cols) setdiff(all_cols, cols))
  names(missing_cols) <- df_names
  return(list(common_variables = common_cols, missing_variables = missing_cols))
}

text:
child_pugh_score
Computes Child-Pugh score from 5 components (bilirubin, albumin, INR, ascites, HE grade).


Code
child_pugh_score <- function(tbili, albumin, inr, ascites, hegrade) {
  childpugh_bili <- ifelse(tbili < 2, 1, ifelse(tbili >= 2 & tbili <= 3, 2, 3))
  childpugh_alb <- ifelse(albumin > 3.5, 1, ifelse(albumin >= 2.8 & albumin <= 3.5, 2, 3))
  childpugh_inr <- ifelse(inr < 1.7, 1, ifelse(inr >= 1.7 & inr <= 2.3, 2, 3))
  childpugh_ascites <- ifelse(ascites == 1, 1, ifelse(ascites == 2, 2, 3))
  childpugh_he <- ifelse(hegrade == 0, 1, ifelse(hegrade %in% c(1, 2), 2, 3))
  childpugh_total <- childpugh_bili + childpugh_alb + childpugh_inr + childpugh_ascites + childpugh_he
  return(childpugh_total)
}

text:
ckd_epi_gfr
Vectorized CKD-EPI 2021 GFR calculation.


Code
ckd_epi_gfr <- function(creat, male, age) {
  k <- ifelse(male == 1, 0.9, 0.7)
  a <- ifelse(male == 1, -0.302, -0.241)
  m <- ifelse(male == 1, 1, 1.012)
  ratio <- creat / k
  gfr <- 142 * (pmin(ratio, 1))^a * (pmax(ratio, 1))^-1.2 * 0.9938^age * m
  return(gfr)
}

title: Data Summary
text:
calculate_clif_c_score
Computes CLIF-C ACLF score from organ sub-scores, age, and WBC.


Code
calculate_clif_c_score <- function(tb_admit, peak_creatinine, he_grade_admission,
                                    inr_admit, map, pressor, fio2_admission,
                                    spo2_admission, age_admission, wbc_admit) {
  clif_score <- 0
  clif_score <- clif_score + case_when(
    is.na(tb_admit) ~ 0, tb_admit < 6 ~ 1,
    tb_admit >= 6 & tb_admit < 12 ~ 2, tb_admit >= 12 ~ 3, TRUE ~ 0)
  clif_score <- clif_score + case_when(
    is.na(peak_creatinine) ~ 0, peak_creatinine < 2 ~ 1,
    peak_creatinine >= 2 & peak_creatinine < 3.5 ~ 2, peak_creatinine >= 3.5 ~ 3, TRUE ~ 0)
  clif_score <- clif_score + case_when(
    is.na(he_grade_admission) ~ 0, he_grade_admission == 1 ~ 1,
    he_grade_admission %in% c(2, 3) ~ 2, he_grade_admission %in% c(4, 5) ~ 3, TRUE ~ 0)
  clif_score <- clif_score + case_when(
    is.na(inr_admit) ~ 0, inr_admit < 2 ~ 1,
    inr_admit >= 2 & inr_admit < 2.5 ~ 2, inr_admit >= 2.5 ~ 3, TRUE ~ 0)
  clif_score <- clif_score + case_when(
    is.na(map) ~ 0, map >= 70 & pressor != 1 ~ 1,
    map < 70 & pressor != 1 ~ 2, !is.na(map) & pressor == 1 ~ 3, TRUE ~ 0)
  if (!is.na(fio2_admission)) {
    ratio <- spo2_admission / fio2_admission
    clif_score <- clif_score + case_when(
      ratio > 357 ~ 1, ratio > 214 & ratio <= 357 ~ 2, ratio <= 214 ~ 3, TRUE ~ 0)
  }
  CLIF_C_Score <- 10 * (0.33 * clif_score + 0.04 * age_admission + 0.63 * log(wbc_admit) - 2)
  return(CLIF_C_Score)
}

title: Data Summary
text:
calculate_organ_failures
Counts ACLF organ failures (0–6) from day 0 labs.


Code
calculate_organ_failures <- function(bili_terli_day0, he_terli_day0, inr_terli_day0,
                                      map_terli_values, fio2_terli_day0,
                                      spo2_terli_day0, scr_terli_day0) {
  liver_f <- as.numeric(!is.na(bili_terli_day0) && bili_terli_day0 >= 6)
  brain_f <- as.numeric(!is.na(he_terli_day0) && he_terli_day0 > 1)
  coagulation_f <- as.numeric(!is.na(inr_terli_day0) && inr_terli_day0 >= 2)
  circulatory_f <- as.numeric(!is.na(map_terli_values) && map_terli_values < 70)
  respiratory_f <- as.numeric(!is.na(fio2_terli_day0) && fio2_terli_day0 != 0 &&
                               !is.na(spo2_terli_day0) && (spo2_terli_day0 / fio2_terli_day0) <= 357)
  kidney_f <- as.numeric(!is.na(scr_terli_day0) && scr_terli_day0 >= 1.5)
  return(sum(liver_f, brain_f, coagulation_f, circulatory_f, respiratory_f, kidney_f))
}

create_table_one
Generates stratified Table 1 and saves as CSV.


Code
create_table_one <- function(all_vars, cat_vars, data, strata, output) {
  numerical_vars <- setdiff(all_vars, cat_vars)
  T1 <- tableone::CreateTableOne(
    vars = all_vars, strata = strata, data = data,
    factorVars = cat_vars, addOverall = TRUE, includeNA = FALSE)
  T1_print <- print(T1, showAllLevels = TRUE, nonnormal = numerical_vars,
    missing = TRUE, quote = FALSE, noSpaces = TRUE, printToggle = FALSE)
  write.csv(T1_print, file = output)
}

  dc_days_to_death = if_else(dc_death_90days == 2 | dc_death_90days == 0, 90, dc_days_to_death)
) %>% mutate(
  days_to_lost_to_followup = if_else(is.na(days_to_lost_to_followup), 0, days_to_lost_to_followup),
  time_to_death = case_when(
    dc_death_status == 1 ~ days_to_discharge,
    dc_death_status == 0 & dc_death_90days == 2 ~ days_to_discharge + days_to_lost_to_followup,
    dc_death_status == 0 & dc_death_90days != 2 ~ days_to_discharge + dc_days_to_death)
) %>% mutate(
  time_to_death_status = case_when(
    dc_death_status == 1 ~ 1,
    dc_death_status == 0 & after_discharge_90days_death_status == 1 ~ 1,
    TRUE ~ 0),
  time_to_death = time_to_death - days_to_terli_initiation_day0)

title: Data Summary
text:
Derive primary variables (Final Data 2)


Code
# Compute population mean bilirubin BEFORE rowwise
bili_pop_mean <- mean(final_master$bili_terli_day0, na.rm = TRUE)

final_master <- final_master %>%
  filter(!is.na(Discont_Day)) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    last_scr = get(paste0("scr_terli_day", Discont_Day)),
    last_scr_2 = if (Discont_Day > 0) get(paste0("scr_terli_day", Discont_Day - 1)) else NA_real_,
    discont_reason_terli = get(paste0("discont_reason_terli_day", Discont_Day)),
    terli_ae_abdpain  = get(paste0("terli_ae_abdpain_day", Discont_Day)),
    terli_ae_nausea   = get(paste0("terli_ae_nausea_day", Discont_Day)),
    terli_ae_diarrhea = get(paste0("terli_ae_diarrhea_day", Discont_Day)),
    terli_ae_volover  = get(paste0("terli_ae_volover_day", Discont_Day)),
    terli_ae_brady    = get(paste0("terli_ae_brady_day", Discont_Day)),
    terli_other       = get(paste0("terli_other_day", Discont_Day))
  ) %>%
  merge(map, by = "subjectid", all.x = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    lowest_creatinine = pmin(last_scr_2, last_scr, scr_day1_post_terli, na.rm = TRUE),
    HRS_reversal_new = case_when(
      lowest_creatinine <= 1.5 & (scr_terli_day0 - lowest_creatinine) >= 0.3 ~ 1, TRUE ~ 0),
    scr_terli_day0 = ifelse(scr_terli_day0 > 100, scr_terli_day0 / 100, scr_terli_day0),
    fio2_terli_day0 = ifelse(fio2_terli_day0 > 1, fio2_terli_day0 / 100, fio2_terli_day0)
  ) %>%
  # Transplant
  mutate(
    transplant_lt = case_when(
      lt_during_hospital == 1 | slkt_during_hospital == 1 ~ 1,
      dc_lt_90days == 1 | dc_lt_90days == 2 ~ 2,
      lt_during_hospital == 0 & lt_status_1yr == 1 & days_to_lt_1yr > 90 ~ 3,
      TRUE ~ 0),
    time_to_transplant = case_when(
      transplant_lt == 1 & !is.na(days_to_lt) ~ days_to_lt,
      transplant_lt == 1 & is.na(days_to_lt) ~ days_to_discharge - days_to_lt_1yr,
      transplant_lt %in% c(2, 3) ~ days_to_discharge + days_to_lt_1yr,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_),
    time_to_transplant = time_to_transplant - days_to_terli_initiation_day0,
    transplant_lt_status = if_else(transplant_lt == 0, 0, 2),
    time_to_transplant_cmp = if_else(is.na(time_to_transplant), 999, time_to_transplant),
    transplant_slkt = case_when(
      slkt_during_hospital == 1 | dc_lt_90days == 2 | lt_type_1yr == 2 ~ 1, TRUE ~ 0)
  ) %>%
  # Baseline SCr
  mutate(
    egfr = 75,
    baseline_scr_calculated = mapply(estimate_creatinine, egfr, age, sex_male),
    baseline_scr_new = case_when(
      baseline_scr_days_to_admit != 0 & !is.na(baseline_scr) ~ baseline_scr,
      baseline_scr_days_to_admit == 0 & !is.na(baseline_scr_120to365_value) ~ baseline_scr_120to365_value,
      baseline_scr_days_to_admit == 0 & is.na(baseline_scr_120to365_value) ~ pmin(baseline_scr_calculated, scr_admit)),
    baseline_scr_new_2 = case_when(
      baseline_scr_days_to_admit != 0 & !is.na(baseline_scr) ~ baseline_scr,
      baseline_scr_days_to_admit == 0 & !is.na(baseline_scr_120to365_value) ~ baseline_scr_120to365_value),
    eGFR = mapply(ckd_epi_gfr, baseline_scr_new_2, sex_male, age)
  ) %>%
  # AKI staging
  mutate(
    ratio = round(scr_terli_day0 / baseline_scr_new, 4),
    delta = round(scr_terli_day0 - baseline_scr_new, 4),
    akin = case_when(
      (ratio >= 1.5 & ratio < 2) | (ratio < 1.5 & delta > 0.3) ~ 1,
      ratio >= 2 & ratio < 3 ~ 2, ratio >= 3 ~ 3, TRUE ~ NA),
    akin = ifelse(scr_terli_day0 >= 4, 3, akin)
  ) %>%
  # BUG #10 FIXED: use pre-computed population mean

  mutate(bili_cal = coalesce(bili_terli_day0, tbili_admit, bili_pop_mean)) %>%
  # Clinical scores (BUG #3 FIXED: bili_terli_day0 instead of tele_terli_day0)
  mutate(
    clif_c_score = mapply(calculate_clif_c_score, tbili_admit, scr_terli_day0, he_terli_day0,
      inr_terli_day0, map_terli_day0_time0, norepi_terli_day0, fio2_terli_day0,
      spo2_terli_day0, age, wbc_terli_day0),
    aclf_grade = mapply(calculate_organ_failures, bili_terli_day0, he_terli_day0, inr_terli_day0,
      map_terli_day0_time0, fio2_terli_day0, spo2_terli_day0, scr_terli_day0),
    child_pugh_score = mapply(child_pugh_score, bili_terli_day0, alb_terli_day0,
      inr_terli_day0, ascites_aki, he_terli_day0)
  ) %>%
  # Discontinuation AKI staging
  mutate(
    discont_ratio = round(lowest_creatinine / baseline_scr_new, 4),
    discont_delta = round(lowest_creatinine - baseline_scr_new, 4),
    discont_aki_stage = case_when(
      is.na(discont_ratio) | is.na(discont_delta) ~ NA_real_,
      discont_ratio < 1.5 & discont_delta < 0.3 ~ 0,
      (discont_ratio >= 1.5 & discont_ratio < 2) | (discont_ratio < 1.5 & discont_delta >= 0.3) ~ 1,
      discont_ratio >= 2 & discont_ratio < 3 ~ 2,
      discont_ratio >= 3 ~ 3),
    discont_aki_stage = ifelse(lowest_creatinine >= 4, 3, discont_aki_stage),
    difference = round(akin - discont_aki_stage, 0),
    hrs_responders = case_when(
      difference <= 0 ~ 0,
      difference > 0 & round(lowest_creatinine - baseline_scr_new, 2) > 0.3 ~ 1,
      round(lowest_creatinine - baseline_scr_new, 2) <= 0.3 ~ 2),
    hrs_responders_cat_2 = ifelse(hrs_responders == 0, 0, 1))

# AE derived variables from discontinuation day
final_master <- final_master %>% rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    terli_7 = if_else(discont_reason_terli == 7, 1, 0),
    terli_8 = if_else(discont_reason_terli == 8, 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  mutate(terli_ae_respfail = if_else(terli_7 == 1 | terli_ae_volover == 1, 1, 0)) %>%
  mutate(across(
    c(terli_ae_abdpain, terli_ae_nausea, terli_ae_diarrhea,
      terli_ae_volover, terli_ae_brady, terli_7, terli_8, terli_ae_respfail),
    ~ if_else(is.na(.x), 0, .x)
  )) %>%
  mutate(
    any_ae = if_else(
      terli_ae_abdpain == 1 | terli_ae_nausea == 1 | terli_ae_diarrhea == 1 |
      terli_ae_volover == 1 | terli_ae_brady == 1 | terli_7 == 1 |
      terli_8 == 1 | terli_ae_respfail == 1, 1, 0),
    listed_transplant = if_else(lt_eval == 2 | lteval_listingstatus == 1, 1, 0),
    listed_transplant = ifelse(is.na(listed_transplant), 0, listed_transplant)
  ) %>% ungroup()

# Dose change status and timing (days 2-12)
dosechange_cols <- c("dosechange_terli_day2", "dosechange_terli_day3",
  "dosechange_terli_day4", "dosechange_terli_day5", "dosechange_terli_day6",
  "dosechange_terli_day7", "dosechange_terli_day8", "dosechange_terli_day9",
  "dosechange_terli_day10", "dosechange_terli_day11", "dosechange_terli_day12")

final_master <- final_master %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    dosechange_status = if_else(any(c_across(all_of(dosechange_cols)) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), 1, 0),
    time_to_dosechange = if_else(
      dosechange_status == 1,
      min(which(c_across(all_of(dosechange_cols)) == 1)) + 1,
      NA_integer_)
  ) %>% ungroup()

final_master <- final_master %>% mutate(
  creatinine_adj_day0 = case_when(scr_terli_day0 < 1 ~ 1, scr_terli_day0 >= 4 ~ 4, TRUE ~ scr_terli_day0),
  day0_meld = round(9.57 * log(creatinine_adj_day0) + 3.78 * log(bili_terli_day0) + 11.20 * log(inr_terli_day0) + 6.43),
  day0_meld_na = round(day0_meld + 1.32 * (137 - pmin(pmax(na_terli_day0, 125), 137)) -
    (0.033 * day0_meld * (137 - pmin(pmax(na_terli_day0, 125), 137)))),
  day0_meld_na = ifelse(day0_meld > 11, day0_meld_na, NA),
  female_factor = if_else(sex_male == 0, 1.33, 0),
  day0_meld_3 = round(female_factor + 4.56*log(bili_terli_day0) + 0.82*(137-na_terli_day0) -
    0.24*(137-na_terli_day0)*log(bili_terli_day0) + 9.09*log(inr_terli_day0) +
    11.14*log(creatinine_adj_day0) + 1.85*(3.5-alb_terli_day0) -
    1.83*(3.5-alb_terli_day0)*log(creatinine_adj_day0) + 6),
  admit_creatinine_adj = case_when(scr_admit < 1 ~ 1, scr_admit >= 4 ~ 4, TRUE ~ scr_admit),
  admit_meld = round(9.57*log(admit_creatinine_adj) + 3.78*log(tbili_admit) + 11.20*log(inr_admit) + 6.43),
  admit_meld_na = round(admit_meld + 1.32*(137-pmin(pmax(sodium_admit,125),137)) -
    (0.033*admit_meld*(137-pmin(pmax(sodium_admit,125),137)))),
  admit_meld_na = ifelse(admit_meld > 11, admit_meld_na, NA),
  admit_meld_3 = round(female_factor + 4.56*log(tbili_admit) + 0.82*(137-sodium_admit) -
    0.24*(137-sodium_admit)*log(tbili_admit) + 9.09*log(inr_admit) +
    11.14*log(admit_creatinine_adj) + 1.85*(3.5-albumin_admit) -
    1.83*(3.5-albumin_admit)*log(admit_creatinine_adj) + 6)
) %>% mutate(
  liver_f = ifelse(!is.na(bili_terli_day0) & bili_terli_day0 >= 6, 1, 0),
  brain_f = ifelse(!is.na(he_terli_day0) & he_terli_day0 > 1, 1, 0),
  coagulation_f = ifelse(!is.na(inr_terli_day0) & inr_terli_day0 >= 2, 1, 0),
  circulatory_f = ifelse(!is.na(map_terli_day0_time0) & map_terli_day0_time0 < 70, 1, 0),
  respiratory_f = ifelse(!is.na(fio2_terli_day0) & fio2_terli_day0 != 0 &
    !is.na(spo2_terli_day0) & (spo2_terli_day0/fio2_terli_day0) <= 357, 1, 0),
  kidney_f = ifelse(!is.na(scr_terli_day0) & scr_terli_day0 >= 1.5, 1, 0),
  organ_failure_sum = liver_f + brain_f + coagulation_f + circulatory_f + respiratory_f + kidney_f
) %>% mutate(
  rrt_2 = ifelse(rrt == 2, 0, rrt),
  aclf_grade = if_else(aclf_grade > 3, 3, aclf_grade),
  criteria_1 = ifelse((total_terli_day1 == 0 | is.na(total_terli_day1)) & discont_terli_day1 == 1, 1, 0),
  criteria_2 = ifelse(aclf_grade >= 3, 1, 0),
  criteria_3 = ifelse(scr_terli_day0 >= 5, 1, 0),
  criteria_4 = case_when(spo2_terli_day0/fio2_terli_day0 < 357 ~ 1, spo2_terli_day0/fio2_terli_day0 >= 357 ~ 0),
  criteria_4 = ifelse(is.na(criteria_4), 0, criteria_4),
  criteria_5 = ifelse(admit_meld_3 > 35, 1, 0),
  HRS_criteria_meld = if_else(criteria_2 == 1 | criteria_3 == 1 | criteria_4 == 1 | criteria_5 == 1, 1, 0),
  HRS_criteria_3 = if_else(criteria_2 == 1 | criteria_3 == 1 | criteria_4 == 1, 1, 0),
  HRS_Criteria = case_when(
    criteria_1 == 0 & criteria_2 == 0 & criteria_3 == 0 & criteria_4 == 0 ~ 0,
    criteria_1 == 1 | criteria_2 == 1 | criteria_3 == 1 | criteria_4 == 1 ~ 1)
)

# 90-day competing risk outcomes
final_master <- final_master %>% mutate(
  time_to_death_90days = pmin(time_to_death, 90),

title: Code Check
section: 1. INU patients assigned MCM ID prefix
text:
1. INU patients assigned MCM ID prefix
Severity: Critical File: Tables.Rmd line 63, revision 0311.Rmd line 63
# Was (WRONG):
inu <- ... %>% mutate(id = paste0("mcm_", row_number()))
# Fixed:
inu <- ... %>% mutate(id = paste0("inu_", row_number()))

title: Code Check
section: 10. mean() inside rowwise() computes single-row mean
text:
10. mean() inside rowwise() computes single-row mean
Severity: Moderate File: Tables.Rmd line 871
# Inside rowwise() context:
bili_cal = coalesce(bili_terli_day0, tbili_admit, mean(bili_terli_day0, na.rm = T))
In rowwise() mode, mean(bili_terli_day0) operates on a single value. If bili_terli_day0 is NA, mean(NA, na.rm=T) returns NaN, not the intended column mean. Compute the column mean before the rowwise() pipe.

title: Code Check
text:
4. estimate_creatinine not vectorized
Severity: Low (latent) File: Tables.Rmd lines 183–207
The function uses scalar if/else and optimize(). Currently called via mapply() which is correct, but the function silently uses only the first element of sex if a vector is ever passed directly.

title: Code Check
text:
20. MELD 3.0: sodium not capped at 125–137
Severity: High File: Tables.Rmd lines 923–933, 943–951
# Current (WRONG):
0.82 * (137 - na_terli_day0) - 0.24 * (137 - na_terli_day0) * log(bili_terli_day0)

# Should be (per Kim et al., Gastroenterology 2023):
0.82 * (137 - pmin(pmax(na_terli_day0, 125), 137)) - ...
The published MELD 3.0 formula caps sodium at 125–137 (same as MELD-Na). Uncapped sodium means hyponatremic patients get inflated scores and hypernatremic patients get paradoxically lower scores.

title: Code Check
text:
21. MELD 3.0: albumin not capped at 1.5–3.5
Severity: High File: Tables.Rmd lines 923–933, 943–951
# Current (WRONG):
1.85 * (3.5 - alb_terli_day0) - 1.83 * (3.5 - alb_terli_day0) * log(creatinine_adj_day0)

# Should use capped albumin:
alb_capped = pmin(pmax(alb_terli_day0, 1.5), 3.5)
1.85 * (3.5 - alb_capped) - 1.83 * (3.5 - alb_capped) * log(creatinine_adj_day0)
MELD 3.0 caps albumin at 1.5–3.5 g/dL. Patients with albumin > 3.5 get a negative albumin contribution, reducing the score.

title: Code Check
section: 22. MELD formulas: bilirubin and INR not floored at 1.0
text:
22. MELD formulas: bilirubin and INR not floored at 1.0
Severity: Moderate File: Tables.Rmd lines 919, 928–929, 939, 948–949
Per UNOS specification, bilirubin, INR, and creatinine should all be floored at 1.0 (set to 1 if < 1). Creatinine is correctly floored (creatinine_adj_day0), but bilirubin and INR are not. log(x) for x < 1 is negative, reducing the score. Rare in HRS but technically incorrect.

title: Code Check
section: 23. CLIF-SOFA kidney failure: threshold should be 2.0, not 1.5
text:
23. CLIF-SOFA kidney failure: threshold should be 2.0, not 1.5
Severity: High File: Tables.Rmd lines 315, 963
# Current (WRONG):
kidney_f = ifelse(!is.na(scr_terli_day0) & scr_terli_day0 >= 1.5, 1, 0)

# Per CLIF-SOFA (Moreau et al., Gastroenterology 2013):
# Kidney failure (CLIF-SOFA = 3) is SCr >= 2.0
# SCr 1.5-1.9 is kidney dysfunction (CLIF-SOFA = 2), NOT failure
kidney_f = ifelse(!is.na(scr_terli_day0) & scr_terli_day0 >= 2.0, 1, 0)
Using ≥ 1.5 overcounts kidney failure, inflating ACLF grades. Directly affects criteria_2 (ACLF ≥ 3) and all futility criteria analyses.

title: Code Check
section: 24. CLIF-SOFA circulatory failure: ignores vasopressor requirement
text:
24. CLIF-SOFA circulatory failure: ignores vasopressor requirement
Severity: Moderate File: Tables.Rmd lines 305, 957
# Current:
circulatory_f = ifelse(!is.na(map_terli_day0_time0) & map_terli_day0_time0 < 70, 1, 0)

# Per CLIF-SOFA: circulatory failure (score=3) = use of vasopressors
# MAP < 70 without vasopressors = score 2 (dysfunction, not failure)
The calculate_clif_c_score function correctly uses MAP + vasopressor logic for sub-scoring, but the organ_failure_sum counts MAP < 70 alone as “failure.” Inconsistent between the two functions.

title: Code Check
section: 25. AKIN Stage 1: delta uses > 0.3 instead of >= 0.3
text:
25. AKIN Stage 1: delta uses > 0.3 instead of >= 0.3
Severity: Moderate File: Tables.Rmd line 865
# Current (WRONG):
akin = case_when(
  (ratio >= 1.5 & ratio < 2) | (ratio < 1.5 & delta > 0.3) ~ 1, ...)
                                                    # ^ should be >=

# Correctly done in discont_aki_stage:
  (discont_ratio < 1.5 & discont_delta >= 0.3) ~ 1  # uses >=
AKIN/KDIGO Stage 1 = increase ≥ 0.3 mg/dL. The initial staging uses > while discontinuation staging correctly uses >=. Patients with delta exactly 0.3 are misclassified at initiation.

title: Code Check
section: 30. race_2: missing race grouped with White
text:
30. race_2: missing race grouped with White
Severity: Moderate (equity concern) File: Tables.Rmd line 1075
race_2 = if_else(race == 1 | is.na(race), 1, 0) – patients with missing race data are classified as White/Caucasian. Could bias adjusted analyses. Consider a separate “unknown” category.

title: Code Check
section: 31. Median imputation for admit_meld_3 and clif_c_score_new
text:
31. Median imputation for admit_meld_3 and clif_c_score_new
Severity: Moderate (design decision) File: Tables.Rmd lines 1071–1074, 1088
Single-value imputation underestimates variance. These imputed versions are used in criteria flags and potentially in models. The multiple imputation in new_reqeust_0311.Rmd does this more correctly.

title: Code Check
section: 32. total_albumin: rowSums(na.rm=TRUE) treats missing days as 0 dose
text:
32. total_albumin: rowSums(na.rm=TRUE) treats missing days as 0 dose
Severity: Moderate (design decision) File: Tables.Rmd lines 1076–1085
A patient with 7 days of data and 7 missing days gets the same total_albumin as a patient who genuinely received 0g on those 7 days. Systematically underestimates albumin exposure for patients with incomplete data.

title: Code Check
section: 34. calculate_clif_c_score parameter named peak_creatinine but receives scr_terli_day0
text:
34. calculate_clif_c_score parameter named peak_creatinine but receives scr_terli_day0
Severity: Low (naming) File: Tables.Rmd line 211
Misleading parameter name. The function is called with day 0 terlipressin creatinine, not peak hospitalization creatinine.

Survival / Competing Risk

Intermediate Variables








Variable
Description
Logic




dc_death_status
In-hospital death
1 if disposition == 5, else 0


after_discharge_90days_death_status
Died within 90 days post-discharge
1 if dc_death_90days == 1, else 0


time_to_death
Days from terlipressin initiation to death or censoring
In-hospital death: days_to_discharge; post-discharge death: days_to_discharge + dc_days_to_death; lost to follow-up: days_to_discharge + days_to_lost_to_followup; alive at 90 days: days_to_discharge + 90 (intentional censoring). All minus days_to_terli_initiation_day0


time_to_death_status
Overall death indicator
1 = died (in-hospital or post-discharge within 90 days), 0 = alive or lost to follow-up





90-Day Death Outcomes









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_death_90days
Follow-up time for 90-day death analysis (days)
0–90
min(time_to_death, 90) – all follow-up capped at 90 days


time_to_death_status_90days
90-day death event indicator
1 = died within 90 days; 0 = alive at 90 days, OR died after 90 days (censored at 90), OR lost to follow-up
1 only if time_to_death_status == 1 AND time_to_death <= 90; all other patients get 0





90-Day Transplant Outcomes









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_transplant_90days
Follow-up time for 90-day transplant analysis (days)
0–90
min(time_to_transplant_cmp, 90)


time_to_transplant_status_90days
90-day transplant event indicator
2 = transplanted within 90 days; 0 = not transplanted within 90 days, OR transplanted after 90 days (censored at 90)
2 only if transplant_lt_status == 2 AND time_to_transplant_cmp <= 90; all other patients get 0





90-Day Competing Risk (Death vs. Transplant)









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_death_90days_cmp
Competing risk follow-up time (days)
0–90
min(time_to_death_90days, time_to_transplant_90days) – whichever event came first


time_to_death_status_90days_cmp
Competing risk event indicator
0 = censored (alive, no transplant within 90 days); 1 = death within 90 days (death came first or tied); 2 = transplant within 90 days (transplant came first)
If death time ≤ transplant time: use death status; if transplant time < death time: use transplant status









NoteHow the competing risk status works






Patient dies day 30, no transplant: time = 30, status = 1 (death)
Patient transplanted day 20, alive at 90 days: time = 20, status = 2 (transplant)
Patient alive at 90 days, no transplant: time = 90, status = 0 (censored)
Patient dies day 50, transplanted day 40: time = 40, status = 2 (transplant came first)
Patient died after 90 days: time = 90, status = 0 (censored – death beyond window)
Patient lost to follow-up day 60: time = 60, status = 0 (censored)







Transplant








Variable
Description
Logic




transplant_lt
LT timing category
1 = During hospitalization (LT or SLKT), 2 = Within 90 days post-discharge, 3 = After 90 days (within 1 year), 0 = No transplant


transplant_slkt
Simultaneous liver-kidney transplant indicator
1 if slkt_during_hospital == 1 OR dc_lt_90days == 2 OR lt_type_1yr == 2, else 0


transplant_lt_status
Transplant status for competing risk
0 = no transplant, 2 = transplanted (coded as 2 for competing risk with death = 1)


time_to_transplant
Days from terlipressin initiation to transplant
NA if no transplant


time_to_transplant_cmp
Transplant time for competing risk (999 if no transplant)
999 serves as a large censoring time so pmin with death time selects death


transplant_outcome
Transplanted within 90 days (binary)
1 if time_to_transplant_status_90days == 2, else 0


listed_transplant
Listed for liver transplant
1 if lt_eval == 2 OR lteval_listingstatus == 1, else 0

title: Derived Variables
text:
Multiple Imputation (revision 0311)
Predictive mean matching (PMM), m=10 imputations, 50 iterations, seed=12345.
Imputed predictors: sex_male, scr_admit, tbili_admit, sodium_admit, inr_admit, albumin_admit, bili_terli_day0, he_terli_day0, inr_terli_day0, map_terli_day0_time0, fio2_terli_day0, spo2_terli_day0, scr_terli_day0, wbc_terli_day0
Post-imputation: Clinical scores (CLIF-C, ACLF grade, Child-Pugh, MELD) are recomputed on each imputed dataset.
Models fit: Fine-Gray competing risk models pooled with Rubin’s rules via run_pooled_fg(), stratified by HRS criteria, transplant listing status, and other subgroups.
